articles.title: Zimbabwe's iconic stone birds were taken by colonialists. Finally, they're all back home
articles.description: For centuries the prized sculptures, central to national identity, have been kept outside Zimbabwe's borders.

articles.title: Mugabe's son pleads guilty to pointing a gun in South Africa
articles.description: Bellarmine Mugabe was arrested in February following the shooting of a 23-year-old man at his home in Johannesburg.

articles.title: Zimbabwe play challenges proposed constitutional changes
articles.description: Bodo is a one-man performance that draws on Ngano, a traditional Shona storytelling method, blending it with modern visual and audio elements to create an immersive experience. Through this fusion of tradition and contemporary theatre, Guzha seeks not only to entertain but to provoke reflection

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: UAE to Exit OPEC+ in Major Energy Policy Shift
more.description: The United Arab Emirates has announced it will withdraw from both OPEC and OPEC+, marking a significant turning point in global energy geopolitics and oil market coordination. According to the state news agency Emirates News Agency, the withdrawal will take effect on May 1, 2026, ending the Gulf nation’s participation in decades of coordinated oil […]The post UAE to Exit OPEC+ in Major Energy Policy Shift appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Zimbabwe’s industrial sector shows tentative recovery as stability returns, says CZI
more.description: HARARE – Zimbabwe’s industrial sector is beginning to register early signs of recovery in 2026, buoyed by improved macroeconomic conditions, policy responsiveness and a renewed emphasis on production and value addition, the Confederation of Zimbabwe Industries (CZI) has reported. In its first-quarter industry outlook, according to State Media, the CZI noted a marked improvement in […]The post Zimbabwe’s industrial sector shows tentative recovery as stability returns, says CZI appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Property developer sparks fresh dispute in Borrowdale
more.description: A Kingsmead Road property in Harare’s upscale Borrowdale neighbourhood has become the subject of a heated legal and public controversy, with property developer Condev Property Developers listing it for US$1.9 million despite an unresolved High Court battle over the land. The dispute pits Condev against Trauma Centre Hospital, which has launched an aggressive bid to […]The post Property developer sparks fresh dispute in Borrowdale appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Waste Management Executive Calls for Immediate Removal of Vendors from Harare City Centre
more.description: HARARE – Mounting pressure is building on authorities to restore order in Harare’s central business district (CBD), as business leaders and urban service providers warn that unchecked informal trading is undermining sanitation systems, formal commerce, and investment confidence. Appearing before the Parliamentary Thematic Committee on Climate Change, Delish Nguwaya, Executive Chairman of Geo Pomona Waste […]The post Waste Management Executive Calls for Immediate Removal of Vendors from Harare City Centre appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Chamisa’s close ally gifted a new Fortuner by Chivayo
more.description: Controversial businessman Wicknell Chivayo has gifted Budiriro North legislator Susan Matsunga a brand new 2026 Toyota Fortuner and pledged US$50,000 for community projects, in a move that has ignited political debate in Zimbabwe. Matsunga, a Citizens Coalition for Change (CCC) Member of Parliament and former ally of Nelson Chamisa, reportedly retained her seat following internal […]The post Chamisa’s close ally gifted a new Fortuner by Chivayo appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Can Zim ditch divisive winner-take-all system for a more inclusive, African-origin one?
more.description: Zimbabwe’s parliament is processing a Constitution Amendment Bill, reopening nationwide discussion about the form of governance most suitable for the country. The article argues that this presents an idea opportunity to ditch the binary, winner-take-all system, which has led to endless crises, and opt for a more inclusive, more African form of government. Can this […]The post Can Zim ditch divisive winner-take-all system for a more inclusive, African-origin one? appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Mnangagwa 2030 bid in bizarre turn
more.description: A constitutional battle over President Emmerson Mnangagwa’s potential term extension has intensified after Parliament Speaker Jacob Mudenda told the Constitutional Court of Zimbabwe that lawmakers lack the power to halt the controversial process. In an answering affidavit, Mudenda argued that only the president has the authority to intervene if the proposed Constitutional Amendment Bill No. […]The post Mnangagwa 2030 bid in bizarre turn appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Procurement inefficiencies driving Zimbabwe fuel prices
more.description: Finance Minister Mthuli Ncube has revealed that inefficiencies in fuel procurement are significantly inflating Zimbabwe’s pump prices, adding up to 40 US cents per litre before taxes are even applied. Speaking during a question-and-answer session at the Zimbabwe International Trade Fair (ZITF), Ncube told delegates that diesel in Zimbabwe carries a cumulative tax burden of […]The post Procurement inefficiencies driving Zimbabwe fuel prices appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: CAB3 could reset presidential clock to 2033: Advocate Thabani Mpofu sounds alarm
more.description: Constitutional lawyer Thabani Mpofu has warned that proposed changes under the Constitution of Zimbabwe (Amendment) Bill (No. 3), 2026 could potentially pave the way for an extension of President Emmerson Mnangagwa’s tenure to 2033, raising fresh debate over the implications of the reforms, and cautioning that even members of Zanu-PF may have been misled about the possible outcome. The post CAB3 could reset presidential clock to 2033: Advocate Thabani Mpofu sounds alarm appeared first on Nehanda Radio .

more.title: Old Mutual signals boardroom shake-up in high-stakes governance reset ahead of AGM
more.description: Old Mutual will implement board committee changes after its June 2026 AGM, as part of a structured succession plan aimed at strengthening governance and oversight. more.title: Police Halt RROZ Operations in Masvingo Over Controversial US$300 Copyright Licence Fees
more.description: Police Halt RROZ Operations in Masvingo Over Controversial US$300 Copyright Licence Fees The Zimbabwe Republic Police (ZRP) has ordered an immediate halt to the operations of the Reprographic Rights Organisation Zimbabwe (RROZ) in Masvingo following mounting complaints from the business community over copyright licence fees. Instructions were issued from Police General Headquarters in Harare directing [...] The post Police Halt RROZ Operations in Masvingo Over Controversial US$300 Copyright Licence Fees appeared first on iHarare News .

more.title: Asset–Liability Mismatch in Zimbabwe’s Pension and Insurance Sector: Why Investment Strategy, Not Just Returns, Is the Real Risk
more.description: IN the technical architecture of banking and insurance, few concepts are as fundamental, and as routinely misunderstood in practice, as immunisation. At its core, immunisation is not about maximising returns; it is about protecting obligations. It is the disciplined alignment of asset duration with liability duration to ensure that future claims, whether pension payouts or [...] The post Asset–Liability Mismatch in Zimbabwe’s Pension and Insurance Sector: Why Investment Strategy, Not Just Returns, Is the Real Risk appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail .

more.title: Marinica resigns as Zimbabwe coach ‘for personal reasons’
more.description: HARARE – Romanian expatriate Marian Marinica resigned as coach of the Zimbabwe national football team on Monday, citing “personal reasons,” ZIFA announced. Assistant coach Kaitano Tembo has been appointed caretaker head coach. Marinica, who took over in November last year, oversaw the Warriors’ unsuccessful campaign at the Africa Cup of Nations finals in Morocco in [...] The post Marinica resigns as Zimbabwe coach ‘for personal reasons’ appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail .

more.title: Beitbridge Husband Kills Wife In Front Of Her Relatives For Trying To Divorce Him
more.description: Husband Snaps During Family Mediation, Wife Fatally Stabbed In Beitbridge A family mediation meant to resolve a troubled marriage ended in tragedy after a man allegedly turned violent and fatally stabbed his wife in Beitbridge on Sunday.
